subject: Computer Networking
title: The Telephone Network
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Telephone Network.

hook.text: In 1876, Bell spoke into a wire and his assistant heard him in the next room. Voice over wire changed everything.

buildup.text: Telephone exchanges connected callers through switchboards. Human operators physically plugged wires together.

discovery.text: The phone system became the largest engineered network in history — circuit-switched and globally connected.

twist.text: Circuit switching reserved a dedicated line for each call. If the line was busy, you simply couldn't connect.

climax.text: This network carried only voice for a century. Nobody imagined it could carry data — until modems came along.

punchline.text: The phone network proved global connectivity was possible.

quiz.question: What switching method did early telephone networks use?
